# Transcrição: Beware_of_the_HIPPO

- **Fonte:** Beware_of_the_HIPPO
- **Duração:** 01:29
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:29]

Yippo, H-I-P-P-O stands for highest paid person's opinion. Any time that person says anything, yes, what they're saying is correct. Surely because they're the most highly paid person in our network. I have to tell you, Ali, you know, hopefully it doesn't come across as being an arrogant ranger, I am one of the most highest paid people in my network. And as a result, people around me always think that I have all the solutions to their problems and they think very highly of my opinion. Just because I've done well in my area of expertise doesn't mean that my advice outside of my area of expertise has any weight at all. And this has been exacerbated online with social media. The number of people that think I can solve their life problems and not their communication skills problems, it's crazy. You are pretty good at solving life problems. Like the fact that you're open enough to say that my knowledge or advice might not actually be correct is probably the reason why I'd actually come to you for more advice. Isn't that interesting? The fact that I said that made you want to ask me for more life advice. I guess the reason I bring this up is because I really want to call that out. I really believe in society. We tend to put people on the pedestal who make a lot of money. I feel that a lot of the times we need to think again with these particular situations because advice is advice based on that person's life, their privileges they were born with, their experiences that they went through, their advantages that they had. We've just got to be so careful with taking advice from people who are doing well financially as being gospel. Just because they're wealthy doesn't mean they have the right answers.
